ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS OFF CALENDAR AT THE MOVING PARTY'S REQUEST. DEFENDANT'S ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO ISSUES 1 AND 2 (GENERAL NEGLIGENCE EXCLUDING NEGLIGENT FAILURE TO WARN, STRICT LIABILITY DESIGN AND MANUFACTURING DEFECT) AND OFF CALENDAR AS TO ISSUE 3 (PREMISES OWNER/CONTRACTOR LIABILITY). AS TO ISSUES 1 AND 2, DEFENDANT SATISFIED ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION OF MAKING A PRIMA FACIE CASE THAT IT IS ENTITLED TO PREVAIL ON ITS GOVERNMENT CONTRACTOR DEFENSE AS TO NON-DUTY TO WARN CLAIMS AND PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRESENTED ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE ISSUE ON THIS DEFENSE. PLAINTIFF HAS DISMISSED HIS PREMISES OWNER/CONTRACTOR LIABILITY CAUSE OF ACTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: PATRICIA RODRIGUEZ OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436
MINI MINUTES FOR SEP-12-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 503

MASTER CALENDAR JURY ASBESTOS
Off Calendar Mar-24-2014 Set For Case Management Conference On Aug-21-14 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 503

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-12-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Felicia Feng of McKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE (415-267-4000), appearing for defendant / moving party Metalclad Insulation Corporation
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party John R. Grant

RULING:
DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation's motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party's request. Defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1 and 2 (general negligence excluding negligent failure to warn, strict liability design and manufacturing defect) and off calendar as to issue 3 (premises owner/contractor liability). As to issues 1 and 2, defendant satisfied its burden of production of making a prima facie case that it is entitled to prevail on its government contractor defense as to non-duty to warn claims and plaintiff has not presented admissible evidence creating a triable issue on this defense. Plaintiff has dismissed his premises owner/contractor liability cause of action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)

DEPT 206 ASBESTOS TRIAL SETTING CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-15-2011 Set For Trial On Oct-22-12 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 503
